transcript: Welcome to Bangkok, one of Kiwi.com's most popular destinations. Bangkok lives and breathes history, from the Grand Palace to its thousands of temples. There's Wat Pho, with its giant reclining Buddha, one of the largest Buddha statues in Thailand, and the huge Wat Arun. Here, if you're nimble, you can climb the steep, narrow central spire for views across the city. Nighttime in Bangkok is sometimes not for the faint-hearted, there's so much going on from the red light district known as Soy Cowboy, named after the cowboy hat wearing American who opened a bar here in the 1970s, to the Ratchada night market and the late night food stands. Speaking of food you'll get your fill in Bangkok. Street food is virtually a religion here with fresh fruit and vegetables, spicy meats and succulent seafood everywhere you turn. Oh, and if you're feeling adventurous, can I interest you in a deep-fried scorpion? Bangkok is built on water, and the rivers and canals that flow through the city also mean transport and trade. Drift down through the floating market where everything from hats to habaneros are on sale from the riverboat retailers. So that's Bangkok, a spicy multicultural mash-up of a city just waiting to be explored.
